title: Discovery Service: Admin Authentication (OIDC/JWT), RBAC, and Session Hardening
description: Implement authentication and authorization for human operators accessing the Discovery Service admin API and UI. Use OIDC for production (with a local JWT dev fallback). Implement role-based access control (RBAC) with four roles: viewer, operator, security-admin, chain-admin. Every admin action is audit-logged with the actor's identity.
objectives: All sensitive admin endpoints protected by validated OIDC/JWT token
Role-to-permission mapping enforced at router dependency layer
Every admin mutation generates an audit_event record with actor ID

steps: Step 1 - Auth Modes: OIDC (production) and local JWT (dev). Controlled by AUTH_MODE env var. In OIDC mode: validate Bearer tokens from configured IdP using JWKS endpoint. In JWT mode: validate tokens signed with a local HS256 key (dev only, never in prod). Fail startup if AUTH_MODE=oidc but OIDC_ISSUER_URL is not set.

Step 2 - OIDC Token Validation: Fetch JWKS from {OIDC_ISSUER_URL}/.well-known/jwks.json (cache for 1h, refresh on key miss). Validate: alg in [RS256, ES256], iss == OIDC_ISSUER_URL, aud contains DISCOVERY_AUDIENCE, exp not expired, nbf not in future. Extract sub (user ID) and roles claim (list of strings).

Step 3 - Role Definitions: VIEWER (read-only access to all resources), OPERATOR (can create/update services, sentinels, issue enrollment tokens), SECURITY_ADMIN (can approve enrollment tokens, issue/revoke credentials, manage trust policy), CHAIN_ADMIN (can publish status anchors, manage blockchain config). Roles stored in JWT custom claim.

Step 4 - RBAC Dependency: Create require_roles(*roles: str) -> Callable FastAPI dependency factory. Returns Depends(verify_token_and_roles). Raises 401 if no valid token, 403 if token lacks required role. Usage: @router.post(..., dependencies=[Depends(require_roles('operator'))]).
Step 5 - CurrentUser Dependency: get_current_user() returns CurrentUser(sub=str, roles=list[str], email=str). Injected via Depends() into all admin endpoints. Used for audit logging.

Step 6 - Audit Integration: Every endpoint that modifies state calls audit_log(session, actor=current_user, action='create_service', target_type='service', target_id=str(service.id), summary={...}). Audit function appends to audit_events table (TASK-022 repository).

Step 7 - Break-glass Admin: A single local emergency account with SECURITY_ADMIN role, credentials stored in Vault (prod) or env var (dev). Access logged with special actor_type='BREAK_GLASS'. Rotated after each use. Documented in TASK-066 runbook.

Step 8 - Session Hardening: No server-side sessions for API clients — stateless JWT Bearer. For UI: access token stored in memory (not localStorage); refresh token via HttpOnly cookie with SameSite=Strict; CSRF token for cookie-bearing requests. Token TTL: access=15min, refresh=8h.
Step 9 - Rate Limiting on Auth: /api/v1/auth/* endpoints rate-limited: 10 requests/minute per IP. Applies to both OIDC callback and dev JWT issue endpoints.

Step 10 - Dev JWT Endpoint (dev mode only): POST /api/v1/auth/dev-token with {sub, roles}. Returns a short-lived JWT signed with LOCAL_JWT_SECRET. ONLY available if DISCOVERY_ENV=dev. Returns 404 in test/prod environments.

fields.name: raw_token
fields.description: Raw JWT for downstream use — never logged

api_endpoints.description: Dev mode only: issue local JWT

error_codes.description: Endpoint not available in this environment

security_constraints: AUTH_MODE=local_jwt MUST NOT be enabled in env=prod or env=test — enforce at startup
JWKS cache MUST be refreshed on key rotation — implement 'kid not found' → re-fetch logic
Refresh tokens MUST use HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Strict cookies
sub from token MUST be recorded in every audit event — never a display name or email
raw_token MUST NOT appear in any log or error response

technical_requirements: python-jose or authlib for JWT validation
Pydantic v2 for token claims validation
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint with require_roles('operator') returns 401 without token
Endpoint with require_roles('operator') returns 403 for viewer-role token
Valid operator token permits access and audit event is written
Dev-token endpoint returns 404 in test/prod environment
Break-glass account has actor_type='BREAK_GLASS' in audit log
